---
title: "example_regenie_gwas_quantanx_metagwas"
author: "Megan Skelton"
output: html_document
---

# QC genotype file or create keep and extract lists to use in REGENIE script
Restrict genotype file to analysis sample  
Perform any necessary additional QC to your genotype file e.g. Minor allele frequency & Hardy-weinberg equilibrium  
Check data format is suitable for REGENIE (MT format) - convert if not
```{bash QC maf hwe}

plink \
-bfile ${gendat}nameofgeneticbfiles \
--keep ${workdir}input/keep_ids.txt \
--maf 0.01 \
--hwe 0.000001 \
--make-bed \
--out ${gendat}nameofgeneticbfiles_quantanx_maf01_hwe6

```

# Regenie Step 1
Create genomic predictions from whole genome regression model using genotyped data with each trait  
https://rgcgithub.github.io/regenie/options/#genetic-data-file-format  

Adjust covar column list as required for your sample (e.g. add array/chip, more PCs) and number of levels for categorical covariates
If you have imputed file only, specify genotyped snps using --extract flag with a list of QCd genotyped snp ids  
Running x-chr analysis separately with sex as an additional covariate
```{bash regenie step 1}

rm ${workdir}scripts/regenie_step1.sh
cat <<'EOT'>> ${workdir}scripts/regenie_step1.sh
#!/bin/bash -l
#SBATCH --partition cpu
#SBATCH --time=0-48:00:00
#SBATCH --nodes=1
#SBATCH --ntasks=8
#SBATCH --mem-per-cpu=19G
#SBATCH --job-name=regenie_step1
#SBATCH --output=/outputdir/regenie_step1_%j.out #replace with your output directory path

gendat=$(sed '1q;d' paths.sh) #fragile - take care references correct path or hardcode absolute paths in the below
workdir=$(sed '2q;d' paths.sh) 

conda activate regenie_env

regenie \
--step 1 \
--bed ${gendat}nameofgeneticbfiles_quantanx_maf01_hwe6 \
--phenoFile ${workdir}input/quantanx_pheno.txt \
--phenoColList latent_fs_std \
--covarFile ${workdir}input/quantanx_covar.txt \
--covarColList batch,PC{1:10} \
--catCovarList batch \
--maxCatLevels 3 \
--bsize 1000 \
--lowmem \
--lowmem-prefix ${workdir}results/regenie_step1/tmp_reg1 \
--out ${workdir}results/regenie_step1/quantanx_regenie_step1 \
--threads 8 \
--gz

EOT

sbatch ${workdir}scripts/regenie_step1.sh

```
   
# Regenie Step 2

Change from vcf as required. If using bed instead of vcf files the dose-field flag can be removed; bed files lose info on quality but should have been filtered previously e.g. INFO >0.3.
This script assumes files are separated by chr, adapt if they are not (will not require array job).  
Running x-chr analysis separately with sex as an additional covariate so only 1-22 here
```{bash regenie step 2}

rm ${workdir}scripts/regenie_step2.sh
cat <<'EOT'>> ${workdir}scripts/regenie_step2.sh
#!/bin/bash -l
#SBATCH --partition cpu
#SBATCH --time=0-48:00:00
#SBATCH --nodes=1
#SBATCH --ntasks=8
#SBATCH --mem-per-cpu=19G
#SBATCH --job-name=regenie_step2
#SBATCH --array=1-22
#SBATCH --output=/outputdir/regenie_step2_%j.out #replace with your output directory path

gendat=$(sed '1q;d' paths.sh) #fragile - take care references correct path or hardcode absolute paths in the below
workdir=$(sed '2q;d' paths.sh) 

CHR=${SLURM_ARRAY_TASK_ID}
echo ${CHR} 

conda activate regenie_env

regenie \
--step 2 \
--vcf ${gendat}nameofgeneticbfiles_imputed_quantanx_chr${CHR}.vcf.gz \
--dose-field DS \
--extract ${gendat}QC/qcd_imp_var_list_chr${CHR}.txt \
--phenoFile ${workdir}input/quantanx_pheno.txt \
--phenoColList latent_fs_std \
--covarFile ${workdir}input/quantanx_covar.txt \
--covarColList batch,PC{1:10} \
--catCovarList batch \
--maxCatLevels 3 \
--write-samples \
--print-pheno \
--bsize 400 \
--pred ${workdir}results/regenie_step1/quantanx_regenie_step1_pred.list \
--out ${workdir}results/regenie_step2/quantanx_regenie_step2_chr${CHR} \
--threads 8 \
--gz

EOT

sbatch ${workdir}scripts/regenie_step2.sh

```

To extect INFO scores from imputed vcfs for downstream steps
```{bash}

for CHR in {1..23}; do
  bcftools query -f '%ID\t%INFO/INFO\n' \
    ${gendat}nameofgeneticbfiles_imputed_quantanx_chr${CHR}.vcf.gz \
    > ${workdir}input/info_scores_chr${CHR}.txt
done

```

If x-chr output is named X as per MT format, rename/read in as 23 for the below script.
Merge chr results.
```{r merge chr output}

source("paths.R")

#ensure installed ("R.utils") so that data.table can read in gzipped files
library(data.table)
library(dplyr)

pheno_name <- "gad_sympt_sev"
n_chr <- 23 #or 22 if running autosomes only
  
# Read and combine all chromosomes
chr_list <- lapply(1:n_chr, function(j) {
  fread(paste0(workdir, "input/quantanx_regenie_step2_chr", j, "_", pheno_name, ".regenie.gz"), header = TRUE)
})

stopifnot("Expected 23 chromosome files" = length(chr_list) == n_chr)
expected_rows <- sum(sapply(chr_list, nrow))

merged <- rbindlist(chr_list)
stopifnot("Row count mismatch after merge" = nrow(merged) == expected_rows)
message("Done merging chromosomes. Total rows: ", nrow(merged))

# Rename, transform P and MAF, and select columns
merged <- merged %>%
  rename(SNP = ID, 
         CHR = CHROM,
         POS = GENPOS,
         EA = ALLELE1,
         NEA = ALLELE0,
         EAF = A1FREQ) %>%
  mutate(P = 10^(-LOG10P),
         MAF = if_else(EAF < 0.5, EAF, 1 - EAF)) %>%
  select(SNP, CHR, POS, EA, NEA, BETA, SE, P, MAF, EAF, N)
 
 stopifnot("P-values out of bounds" = min(merged$P, na.rm=TRUE) > 0 & max(merged$P, na.rm=TRUE) < 1)
message("Done. Final row count: ", nrow(merged))
print(head(merged))

info <- lapply(1:23, function(j) {
  fread(paste0(workdir, "input/info_scores_chr", j, ".txt"),
        col.names = c("SNP", "INFO"))
}) %>% rbindlist()

# Replacing NAs with -999
merged <- merged %>% 
  left_join(info, by = "SNP") %>%
  mutate(across(everything(), ~replace(., is.na(.), -999)))

# save merged sumstats file
write.table(merged, 
              "results/quant_anx_GWAS.txt", 
              sep = "\t", 
              row.names = FALSE, 
              col.names = TRUE,
              quote = FALSE)

```
